coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 6–8 miles, Z2 only (120–135 bpm), mostly flat or rolling. You're 82 days out and in week 22 taper—this is about staying sharp without accumulating fatigue before the Julian Alps push. Your recovery score is 85/100 and sleep last night was 8.23h at 89% efficiency; you're in good shape to absorb one more solid week. Save any tempo or vert for Tuesday or Wednesday. Sunday is your second long effort window, but in taper, that means keeping the aerobic engine warm, not testing it.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Get 60–80g carbs + 20g protein within 30 min of finishing. On an 8-mile easy run you won't need gels, but front-load breakfast: oats, fruit, some fat. You're traveling constantly for work; pack a recovery bottle (sports drink + whey isolate) so you don't skip refuel during airport layovers. Taper weeks are when small fueling slips compound—your glycogen stores are the baseline fitness you've built over 22 weeks. Don't waste it by eating poorly on Sundays.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Sleep is your north star right now: keep targeting 8+ hours and protect it like a race. Your 30-day average is 6.58h, which is undershooting for someone at your training load—last night's 8.23h was an anomaly, not a trend. In taper, consistency matters more than heroic mileage. Add 15–20 min of easy mobility (hips, ankles, calves) post-run; Julian Alps is rocky and technical, and ankle stability under fatigue is non-negotiable. No massage gun aggression today.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 11.7 weeks from Kranjska Gora. The heavy lifting is done—week 20–21 peaked your system. Now your job is *retention*, not growth. Taper anxiety is real for 50K athletes; expect your legs to feel flat for 1–2 more weeks as your central nervous system downregulates. That's not weakness; it's adaptation. Review your race plan once: aid stations, vert profile splits, shoe/sock setup on technical descents. Mental prep now beats scrambling in September.
